message:
feat(perf): define performance budgets + metrics with enforcement tests (bd-1fc4)

Add 13 performance budgets covering startup, extension loading, tool
call latency/throughput, event dispatch, policy evaluation, memory,
binary size, and protocol parsing. Each budget has explicit thresholds,
measurement methodology, and CI enforcement flags.

7 tests validate budget definitions, uniqueness, CI coverage, and check
actual measurements against thresholds. 5 budgets pass with data, 8
await benchmark runs. Generates PERF_BUDGETS.md, budget_summary.json,
and budget_events.jsonl reports.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -530,7 +530,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-rhl","title":"Remove tokio and reqwest dependencies","description":"# Remove tokio and reqwest dependencies

## Goal
Complete the HTTP migration by removing tokio and reqwest from Cargo.toml.

## Background
After all providers migrate to asupersync, these dependencies become unused:
- tokio (async runtime)
- tokio-stream (stream utilities)
- reqwest (HTTP client)

This reduces binary size and simplifies the dependency tree.

## Implementation Steps

1. **Update Cargo.toml**
```toml
# Remove these lines:
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tokio-stream = "0.1"
reqwest = { version = "0.13", ... }

# Ensure asupersync is enabled (currently commented out):
asupersync = { path = "../asupersync", features = ["tls", "tls-native-roots", "sqlite"] }
```

2. **Update main.rs**
```rust
// Before
#[tokio::main]
async fn main() { ... }

// After - use asupersync runtime
fn main() {
    asupersync::runtime::Runtime::new()
        .block_on(async_main())
}
```

3. **Update all async code**
- Replace tokio::spawn with asupersync::spawn
- Replace tokio::time::sleep with asupersync equivalents
- Replace tokio::select! with asupersync patterns
- Update channel usage (if tokio channels used)

4. **Update test harness**
```rust
// Before
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_foo() { ... }

// After
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_foo() { ... }
```

## Files Affected
- Cargo.toml
- src/main.rs
- src/agent.rs (spawn, select)
- src/tools.rs (bash timeout)
- All test files

## Dependencies
- bd-37l (Anthropic migrated)
- bd-1vo (OpenAI migrated)
- bd-1iy (Gemini migrated)
- bd-ivj (Azure migrated)

## Testing
- cargo build must succeed without tokio
- All tests must pass
- Binary size should decrease (~500KB-1MB)

## Risks
- Some tokio-specific features may need alternatives
- Test harness changes may be extensive

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tokio not in Cargo.toml
- [ ] reqwest not in Cargo.toml
- [ ] cargo build succeeds
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Binary size reduced","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:32.853980666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:24:53.392590688Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T08:37:48.931668013Z","close_reason":"Completed: tokio/reqwest fully removed; tests+clippy green","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rhl","depends_on_id":"bd-1iy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-rhl","depends_on_id":"bd-1vo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-rhl","depends_on_id":"bd-37l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-rhl","depends_on_id":"bd-gi3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-rhl","depends_on_id":"bd-ivj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-rhyl","title":"Define scoring rubric + weights","description":"# Goal
Create a transparent scoring model to rank extensions fairly and consistently.

# Deliverables
- Weighted criteria: popularity (downloads/stars), recency, maintenance activity, license permissiveness, runtime compatibility, reliability risk.
- Clear normalization rules and tie‑breakers.
- Documented rationale for each weight.

# Notes
This rubric must be simple enough to apply manually yet defensible.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:16.187391961Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:11:51.613898586Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rhyl","depends_on_id":"bd-3o8d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:16.202684254Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-rhyl","depends_on_id":"bd-97qh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:07:36.901909784Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":331,"issue_id":"bd-rhyl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: Popularity alone is not enough; we need a balanced evaluation across usability and maintainability.

Reasoning: A weighted rubric makes selection reproducible and defensible.

Considerations: Keep the rubric simple, transparent, and aligned with Pi runtime constraints.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:49:37Z"},{"id":381,"issue_id":"bd-rhyl","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"MAKE THIS EXECUTABLE + TESTED

To avoid “hand-wavy” selection, implement the rubric as a deterministic scoring function and test it.

Requirements
- Score breakdown per criterion + total score.
- Explicit handling of missing data (unknown != 0).
- Marketplace metrics support (blocked on bd-97qh).

Tests
- Unit tests for normalization + tie-breakers.
- Golden examples (3+) asserted exactly.
- Offline E2E: run scoring over a fixture candidate pool and emit a stable ranked list.

Logging
- Emit a run summary (histograms + top-N lists + any manual overrides flagged).
","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:11:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-s1hx","title":"Task: Implement input event (transforming, blocking)","description":"# Task: Implement Input Event (Transforming, Blocking)

## Objective

Dispatch the input event before processing user messages, allowing extensions to transform or block input.

## TypeScript Reference

```typescript
if (runner.hasHandlers('input')) {
    const inputResult = await runner.emit({
        type: 'input',
        content: userMessage.content,
        attachments: userMessage.attachments,
    });
    
    if (inputResult?.block) {
        console.log(inputResult.reason || 'Input blocked');
        return;
    }
    
    if (inputResult?.content) {
        userMessage.content = inputResult.content;
    }
}
```

## Event Details

### input Event
- **When**: Before processing user message
- **Purpose**: Transform, filter, or block input
- **Data**: content, attachments

### Return Value
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Deserialize)]
pub struct InputEventResult {
    pub content: Option<String>,      // Transformed content
    pub block: bool,                   // If true, block processing
    pub reason: Option<String>,        // Reason for blocking
}
```

## Implementation

```rust
impl Agent {
    async fn process_user_input(
        &mut self,
        content: String,
        attachments: Vec<Attachment>,
    ) -> Result<Option<UserMessage>> {
        if let Some(dispatcher) = &self.event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("input") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::Input {
                    content: content.clone(),
                    attachments: attachments.clone(),
                };
                
                if let Some(result) = dispatcher.dispatch::<InputEventResult>(event).await? {
                    if result.block {
                        let reason = result.reason.unwrap_or_else(|| "Input blocked".to_string());
                        self.display_message(&reason)?;
                        return Ok(None);
                    }
                    
                    let final_content = result.content.unwrap_or(content);
                    return Ok(Some(UserMessage { content: final_content, attachments }));
                }
            }
        }
        
        Ok(Some(UserMessage { content, attachments }))
    }
}
```

## Use Cases

1. **Macro Expansion**: Replace !date with current date
2. **Content Filter**: Block PII
3. **Translation**: Auto-translate input
4. **Logging**: Record all user inputs

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (5 cases)
1. test_no_handlers_uses_original
2. test_handler_returns_null_uses_original
3. test_handler_transforms_content
4. test_handler_blocks_input
5. test_block_reason_displayed

### E2E Test Script
Extension with macro expansion and content filter, JSONL logging.

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-jt3k (Wire Hostcall Dispatcher)
- Part of: bd-10vp (Extension Event Hook Dispatch)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] input event dispatched before processing
- [ ] Transformation applied correctly
- [ ] Blocking works with reason display
- [ ] 5 unit tests pass
- [ ] E2E test passes","notes":"Progress 2026-02-05: Implemented input event transform/block handling in interactive + AgentSession (apply_input_event_response helper, UpdateLastUserMessage for UI), removed duplicate input dispatch, and ensured before_agent_start dispatch still runs. Fixed clippy/test warnings in tests/provider_factory.rs, tests/agent_loop_vcr.rs, tests/e2e_cli.rs, tests/compaction.rs. Gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings. cargo test: 1 failure (e2e_interactive_smoke_tmux timeout)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:58:38.527571457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T03:27:40.087499583Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T03:27:40.087436135Z","close_reason":"Implemented input event (transform/block) dispatch in interactive + AgentSession; tests/gates green","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-s1hx","depends_on_id":"bd-10vp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:58:38.534290007Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-s1hx","depends_on_id":"bd-jt3k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:58:59.201969470Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-s1hx","depends_on_id":"bd-tg4w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:58:58.239110985Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-sas4","title":"Event dispatch latency benchmarks (p99 less than 5ms)","description":"# Event dispatch latency benchmarks (p99 < 5ms)

## What
Fire 1000+ events of each type through both runtimes, measure handler response time.

## Events to Benchmark
All ExtensionEvent types: tool_call, tool_result, turn_start, turn_end, before_agent_start, input, context, resources_discover, user_bash, session_before_compact, session_before_tree.

## Method
1. Load a test extension that subscribes to all event types
2. Fire 1000 events per type with varied payloads
3. Measure time from dispatch to handler return
4. Compute P50, P95, P99 for each event type in each runtime

## Target
- P99 < 5ms for all event types in Rust
- Rust P50 < TypeScript P50 (should be faster due to native code)

## Output
- Latency report with percentile breakdown per event type","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:21.013754536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T19:26:26.131409126Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T19:26:26.131325280Z","close_reason":"Implemented TS+Rust event dispatch latency benchmark harness and ignored report generator.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sas4","depends_on_id":"bd-139x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:21.019509316Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-sas4","depends_on_id":"bd-150s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:21.027457715Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-slpn","title":"Define refresh cadence + triggers","description":"# Goal
Establish how often and why we re‑run extension research and validation.

# Deliverables
- Cadence (e.g., quarterly) and trigger events (major ecosystem changes).
- Criteria for emergency refresh (security incidents, major breakages).
- Ownership/responsibility notes for running refreshes.

# Notes
Keep cadence realistic and sustainable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:41:56.629827657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:54:06.564320435Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-slpn","depends_on_id":"bd-26xo","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:41:57.039490026Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":359,"issue_id":"bd-slpn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: The extension landscape changes quickly and needs regular review.

Reasoning: A defined cadence and triggers make refreshes predictable and defensible.

Considerations: Balance freshness with the cost of running the full validation pipeline.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:54:06Z"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,94 @@
+# Performance Budgets
+
+> Generated: 2026-02-06T01:24:50Z
+
+## Summary
+
+| Metric | Value |
+|---|---|
+| Total budgets | 13 |
+| CI-enforced | 8 |
+| PASS | 5 |
+| FAIL | 0 |
+| No data | 8 |
+
+## Startup
+
+| Budget | Metric | Threshold | Actual | Status | CI |
+|---|---|---|---|---|---|
+| `startup_version_p95` | p95 latency | 100 ms | - | NO_DATA | Yes |
+| `startup_full_agent_p95` | p95 latency | 200 ms | - | NO_DATA | No |
+
+## Extension
+
+| Budget | Metric | Threshold | Actual | Status | CI |
+|---|---|---|---|---|---|
+| `ext_cold_load_simple_p95` | p95 cold load time | 5 ms | 0.8 | PASS | Yes |
+| `ext_cold_load_complex_p95` | p95 cold load time | 50 ms | 0.6 | PASS | No |
+| `ext_load_60_total` | total load time (60 official extensions) | 10000 ms | 6198.0 | PASS | No |
+
+## Tool_call
+
+| Budget | Metric | Threshold | Actual | Status | CI |
+|---|---|---|---|---|---|
+| `tool_call_latency_p99` | p99 per-call latency | 200 us | 44 | PASS | Yes |
+| `tool_call_throughput_min` | minimum calls/sec | 5000 calls/sec | 22883 | PASS | Yes |
+
+## Event_dispatch
+
+| Budget | Metric | Threshold | Actual | Status | CI |
+|---|---|---|---|---|---|
+| `event_dispatch_p99` | p99 dispatch latency | 5000 us | - | NO_DATA | No |
+
+## Policy
+
+| Budget | Metric | Threshold | Actual | Status | CI |
+|---|---|---|---|---|---|
+| `policy_eval_p99` | p99 evaluation time | 500 ns | - | NO_DATA | Yes |
+
+## Memory
+
+| Budget | Metric | Threshold | Actual | Status | CI |
+|---|---|---|---|---|---|
+| `idle_memory_rss` | RSS at idle | 50 MB | - | NO_DATA | Yes |
+| `sustained_load_rss_growth` | RSS growth under 30s sustained load | 5 percent | - | NO_DATA | No |
+
+## Binary
+
+| Budget | Metric | Threshold | Actual | Status | CI |
+|---|---|---|---|---|---|
+| `binary_size_release` | release binary size | 20 MB | - | NO_DATA | Yes |
+
+## Protocol
+
+| Budget | Metric | Threshold | Actual | Status | CI |
+|---|---|---|---|---|---|
+| `protocol_parse_p99` | p99 parse+validate time | 50 us | - | NO_DATA | Yes |
+
+## Measurement Methodology
+
+- **`startup_version_p95`**: hyperfine: `pi --version` (10 runs, 3 warmup)
+- **`startup_full_agent_p95`**: hyperfine: `pi --print '.'` with full init (10 runs, 3 warmup)
+- **`ext_cold_load_simple_p95`**: criterion: load_init_cold for simple single-file extensions (10 samples)
+- **`ext_cold_load_complex_p95`**: criterion: load_init_cold for multi-registration extensions (10 samples)
+- **`ext_load_60_total`**: conformance runner: sequential load of all 60 official extensions
+- **`tool_call_latency_p99`**: pijs_workload: 2000 iterations x 1 tool call, release build
+- **`tool_call_throughput_min`**: pijs_workload: 2000 iterations x 10 tool calls, release build
+- **`event_dispatch_p99`**: criterion: event_hook dispatch for before_agent_start (100 samples)
+- **`policy_eval_p99`**: criterion: ext_policy/evaluate with various modes and capabilities
+- **`idle_memory_rss`**: sysinfo: measure RSS after startup, before any user input
+- **`sustained_load_rss_growth`**: stress test: 15 extensions, 50 events/sec for 30 seconds
+- **`binary_size_release`**: ls -la target/release/pi (stripped)
+- **`protocol_parse_p99`**: criterion: ext_protocol/parse_and_validate for host_call and log messages
+
+## CI Enforcement
+
+CI-enforced budgets are checked on every PR. A budget violation blocks the PR from merging. Non-CI budgets are informational and checked in nightly runs.
+
+```bash
+# Run budget checks
+cargo test --test perf_budgets -- --nocapture
+
+# Generate full budget report
+cargo test --test perf_budgets generate_budget_report -- --nocapture
+```
